frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre, aajkal ke ladke bhi na, ladki ne mana kiya toh mazaak samajhte hain. Mere bhatije ne bhi ek girl ke peeche kaafi din tak try kiya, phir pata chala uska boyfriend hai toh turant hatt gaya. Maine bhi kaha, wahi theek hai, jab kisi ke saath ho toh respect karna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it presents backing off only after learning the woman has a boyfriend as the proper reason to stop, rather than respecting her own refusal. It encodes the surface manifestation of treating a male attachment as more legitimate than the woman's direct disinterest.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
